element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Single-lap circuit races against 20 AI opponents
Slippery roads (winter), puddles, wooden logs, road blocks depending on environment
Strategic pit stops for refuelling during races
Navigation of elevation changes that affect speed
Increasing difficulty indicated by density of roadside turn markers

typical_tension_triggers: Complex turn chains (indicated by dense roadside signs)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Arcade-oriented rhythmic tension through turn complexity and hazard density, with natural relief through straightaways and pit stops

spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Roadside sign density communicates turn difficulty - more signs indicate sharper/more complex turns

difficulty_curve.early_game: Three race series structure with progressive difficulty levels; weaker players given advantage through reverse starting positions based on previous race placement
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Increased hazard density and complex turn sequences; fuel management becomes critical strategic element
difficulty_curve.late_game: Most complex turn patterns; higher concentration of hazards; tighter AI opponent competition
notable_spikes: Transition between race series (increasing opponent skill and hazard density)
Introduction of hazard-specific courses (winter slippery roads, motorway traffic)

notable_level_patterns.description: Each track consists of lap-based circuits with varying turns, hills, and hollows. Turn difficulty communicated through chain of roadside signs. Fuel management required via pit stops.
Hazards vary by environment context - winter courses feature slippery surfaces, other courses feature puddles/logs/roadblocks. Spatial environment signals obstacle type.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Motorway level (cross-traffic hazard) in Amiga version

notable_level_patterns.description: Three race series with increasing difficulty levels. Qualification through top-10 placement grants progression and high-score points. Starting position inverted from previous finish to support skill progression.
